title: String functions
summary: String functions in Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions

sketchBlocks.title: What are string functions in SQL?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: String functions in SQL allow you to manipulate text data in your queries. Common string functions include `UPPER()`, `LOWER()`, `LENGTH()`, and `SUBSTR()`. These functions can help you clean, format, or extract information from text columns. For example, you can use `UPPER(customer_name)` to display all customer names in uppercase.

sketchBlocks.title: Using string functions in SELECT
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can use string functions directly in the SELECT clause to create calculated columns. For example, to show the length of each customer's name in the `orders` table:

```sql
SELECT customer_name, LENGTH(customer_name) AS name_length FROM orders;
```
This will return each customer name along with the number of characters in their name.

sketchBlocks.title: Combining string functions and aliases
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
String functions are often combined with aliases to make results easier to read. For example, to display the first three letters of each region in uppercase:

```sql
SELECT region, UPPER(SUBSTR(region, 1, 3)) AS region_code FROM orders;
```
This query creates a new column `region_code` showing the first three letters of the region in uppercase.

quizDraft.title: Show customer names in uppercase
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to display each customer's name in uppercase along with their original name from the `orders` table.
quizDraft.hint: Use the UPPER() function on the customer_name column.
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT customer_name, UPPER(customer_name) AS customer_name_upper FROM orders;

help.concept: The UPPER() function converts text to uppercase. You can use it in the SELECT clause to transform column values.
help.hint_1: Apply UPPER() to the customer_name column and use an alias for clarity.
help.hint_2: SELECT customer_name, UPPER(customer_name) AS ... FROM orders;

quizDraft.title: Extract region code
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to show each region and the first two letters of the region in lowercase, labeled as `region_code`.
quizDraft.hint: Use SUBSTR() to get part of the string and LOWER() to change case.
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT region, LOWER(SUBSTR(region, 1, 2)) AS region_code FROM orders;

help.concept: You can nest string functions like SUBSTR() and LOWER() to extract and format parts of text columns.
help.hint_1: First use SUBSTR(region, 1, 2) to get the first two letters, then wrap it in LOWER().
help.hint_2: Try LOWER(SUBSTR(region, 1, 2)) as the calculated column.

quizDraft.title: Purpose of the LENGTH() function
quizDraft.prompt: What does the LENGTH() function return when used on a text column in SQL?
quizDraft.hint: It counts something about the text.

options.content: The number of characters in the text
The number of words in the text
The number of rows in the table
The position of the first letter

help.concept: LENGTH() returns the count of characters in a string, not words or rows.
help.hint_1: Think about how many letters or symbols are in the text.
help.hint_2: LENGTH() is about the size of the text value, not the table.

quizDraft.title: Which are valid string functions in SQLite?
quizDraft.prompt: Select all functions below that are valid string functions in SQLite.
quizDraft.hint: Some functions change case, others extract parts of text.

help.concept: String functions operate on text values. SUM() is an aggregate numeric function, not a string function.
help.hint_1: Look for functions that work with text, not numbers.
help.hint_2: UPPER, LOWER, SUBSTR, and LENGTH are all for strings.

quizDraft.title: Function to extract part of a string
quizDraft.prompt: Which function extracts a substring from a text value in SQLite?
quizDraft.hint: It's often used to get part of a string.
quizDraft.template: To get the first three letters of a region, use ____ (region, 1, 3).

help.concept: SUBSTR() extracts a portion of a string, starting at a given position and for a given length.
help.hint_1: It's not LENGTH, UPPER, or SUM.
help.hint_2: The function starts with 'SUB' and is used for substrings.
